rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PHILIP M. EISENBERG TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs' Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order (1) Preliminarily Approving Class Action Settlement;(2) Provisionally Certifying Distribution Of Notice Of Settlement To The Class; And (4) Setting Heating Fir Final Approval.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 1, 2012, Line 1. PLAINTIFFS THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, ROBERT MESSICK AND BLIND BABIES FOUNDATION'S Notice of Motion And Motion For Order (1) Preliminarily Approving Class Action Settlement;(2) Provisionally Certifying Distribution Of Notice Of Settlement To The Class; And (4) Setting heating For Final Approval. Granted. The settlement appears to be in the range of possible approval at a final hearing. Hearing required to establish future dates. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA TOW TRUCK ASSOCIATION VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings; Memo Of P & A And Req. For Judicial Ntc And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 1, 2012, Line 4. PLAINTIFF CALIFORNIA TOW TRUCK'S ASSOCIATION Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Denied. Plaintiff's facial challenge is denied. U.S. v. Clinical Leasing Service, Inc. (5th Cir. 1991) 925 F.2d 120, 123 is persuasive authority supporting defendant's position. Defendant contends that the towing permit scheme is only being applied to those drivers and firms that conduct substantial or consequential business in San Francisco. Plaintiff has leave to amend to allege, if it can do so in good faith, that the towing permit scheme is being applied to drivers and firms that perform minimal or transitory work in the City. Plaintiff also has leave to amend to allege, if it can do so in good faith, that the permit fees are impermissible taxes, i.e. the fees are applied for general revenue purposes or are unreasonably excessive when compared to the regulating activity. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

Defendant contends that the towing permit scheme is only being applied to those drivers and firms that conduct substantial or consequential business in San Francisco. Plaintiff has leave to amend to allege, if it can do so in good faith, that the towing permit scheme is being applied to drivers and firms that perform minimal or transitory work in the City. Plaintiff also has leave to amend to allege, if it can do so in good faith, that the permit fees are impermissible taxes, i.e. the fees are applied for general revenue purposes or are unreasonably excessive when compared to the regulating activity. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: KWANG HYUN KIM VS. CHE KYUNG PARK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Order Compelling Responses To Special Ineroggs 9set Two) And Mtn For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 1, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF KWANG KIM's Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Special Interrogatories (set Two) and motion For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion is OFF CALENDAR. Failure to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BUNZL DISTRIBUTION USA, INC., A DELAWARE VS. FRANCHISE TAX BOARD, AN AGENCY OF THE STATE OF
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 1, 2012, Line 7. DEFENDANT FRANCHISE TAX BOARD'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Granted as to both the first and second causes of action. The withholding of consent to California jurisdiction by a non-resident member of a single member LLC on an information return per Revenue and Taxation Code section 18633.5 does not obviate the requirement to pay California income tax based on the LLC apportionment factors pursuant to the Uniform Division of Income for Tax Purposes Act. rulings.Case Title: CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL, LTD., A FOREIGN CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 1, 2012, Line 8. CROSS DEFENDANT TOWERS WATSON AMERICA LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Sustained with fifteen days leave to amend. The fifth and sixth causes of action in the Cross Complaint are uncertain and fail to state facts sufficient to constitute claims for indemnity. Incorporation by reference of a complaint filed in another action is not a permissible way to allege necessary facts. Cross Complainant is given leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege facts sufficient to constitute causes of action for full or partial indemnity. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CHABAD OF S. FRANCISCO, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. ABRAHAM AMAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Sanctions Against Deft And Their Attorneys
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 1, 2012, Line 22. PLAINTIFF CHABAD OF S. FRANCISCO, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S Notice Of Motion For Sanctions Against Defendants And Their Attorneys. Granted in part. Defendants may not introduce any evidence at trial except for what they have included in their most recent supplemental responses. Both sides' requests for monetary sanctions are denied. Plaintiff's failure to engage in any meet and confer before filing the motion precludes an award of monetary sanctions in its favor. Defendants' service of supplemental responses after the filing of the motion precludes an award of monetary sanctions in their favor. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 1, 2012, Line 20. Plaintiff City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction. Granted.
